steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with "Though it was designated as mission" — a dependent clause that needs a subject to complete the phrase after the mission name. The blank must connect the mission name STS-45 to the independent clause "the mission was actually the forty-sixth flight" using correct boundary punctuation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'STS-45, the': A comma after the dependent clause opener correctly separates the subordinate clause from the independent clause that follows, and "the" begins the main clause subject. ✓
  • Choice B — 'STS-45, and the': Adding the coordinating conjunction "and" after the comma creates an awkward construction — "and" is used to join two independent clauses, but the first part is a dependent clause introduced by "Though," so "and" is illogical here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'STS-45. The': A period creates a sentence fragment: "Though it was designated as mission STS-45" cannot stand alone as a complete sentence because "Though" makes it dependent. ✗
  • Choice D — 'STS-45 the': Omitting punctuation entirely fuses the dependent clause directly into the independent clause with no boundary, creating a run-on. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
